steps.title: Use the intercept point
steps.content:
The line has slope $\frac{5}{6}$ and passes through the $x$-intercept $(18,0)$.
$$
y-0=\frac{5}{6}(x-18)
$$

steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content: Rewrite in slope-intercept form. $$ y=\frac{5}{6}x-\frac{5}{6}(18) $$ $$ y=\frac{5}{6}x-15 $$ So the $y$-coordinate of the $y$-intercept is $-15$. The answer is -15.
